content.text: The genitive case indicates possession. In English, we often use the particle 'of' to show possession, for example: 'He is the husband of my sister.'
While this construction is not natural English — we would usually say 'He is my sister’s husband' — it demonstrates that the genitive case also exists in English.
Here are examples of first declension nouns in the genitive case:
The –ης ending is the first declension genitive singular ending.
